courses.title: Agentic AI in Supply Chain
courses.summary: Autonomous agents for resilient and intelligent supply chains.
courses.description: A specialised course on designing, evaluating and governing AI agents across planning, procurement, logistics, inventory and supply-chain control processes.
courses.audience: Supply-chain, operations and engineering management students and professionals exploring intelligent workflow automation.
courses.prerequisites: Introductory knowledge of supply-chain processes; prior AI experience is not required.
outcomes: Map supply-chain decisions suitable for AI-agent support.
Design agent roles, tools, data access and human controls.
Evaluate reliability, coordination and operational risk.
Develop performance measures for agent-enabled workflows.
Propose a phased implementation for a supply-chain use case.

modules.title: Supply-chain decisions and agent opportunities
modules.description: Planning, procurement, inventory, logistics and service.

modules.title: Agent design and workflow orchestration
modules.description: Roles, tools, memory, hand-offs and escalation.

modules.title: Data and enterprise-system integration
modules.description: Operational data, APIs and process constraints.

modules.title: Reliability, controls and governance
modules.description: Human oversight, exception handling and accountability.

modules.title: Pilot design and scale-up
modules.description: Metrics, operating change and implementation roadmap.

courses.title: AI for Business
courses.summary: AI concepts and practical applications for business leaders.
courses.description: A managerial introduction to artificial intelligence focused on use-case selection, business value, operating implications, risk and implementation choices.
courses.audience: Managers, analysts, consultants and business students who need to evaluate and lead AI-enabled initiatives.
courses.prerequisites: No programming prerequisite. Familiarity with business processes is expected.
outcomes: Explain major AI capabilities in business terms.
Identify and prioritise viable business use cases.
Evaluate data, technology and organisational readiness.
Assess value, risk and implementation alternatives.
Develop a practical AI adoption roadmap.

modules.title: Understanding AI in business
modules.description: Capabilities, limitations and the evolving business landscape.

modules.title: Use-case discovery and prioritisation
modules.description: Value pools, feasibility and portfolio choices.

modules.title: Data, platforms and operating models
modules.description: Foundations for implementation and scale.

modules.title: Generative and autonomous AI applications
modules.description: Business workflows, human oversight and performance.

modules.title: Implementation roadmap
modules.description: Governance, change, measurement and continuous improvement.

courses.title: AI for Manufacturing Engineering
courses.summary: AI techniques for smart and efficient manufacturing systems.
courses.description: An applied course linking artificial intelligence with manufacturing engineering decisions, process performance, quality, maintenance and production systems.
courses.audience: Manufacturing, industrial, mechanical and engineering management students and professionals.
courses.prerequisites: Basic manufacturing systems knowledge and introductory statistics.
outcomes: Identify manufacturing decisions that can benefit from AI.
Prepare sensor and production data for analysis.
Evaluate quality, maintenance and process applications.
Connect AI models with manufacturing workflows and controls.
Design an implementation proposal for a smart manufacturing use case.

modules.title: AI opportunities in manufacturing
modules.description: Production, quality, maintenance, energy and flow.

modules.title: Industrial data foundations
modules.description: Sensors, historians, traceability and data quality.

modules.title: Predictive quality and maintenance
modules.description: Failure patterns, anomaly detection and decision thresholds.

modules.title: Process optimisation and intelligent control
modules.description: Operational recommendations and closed-loop considerations.

modules.title: Smart manufacturing implementation
modules.description: Architecture, workforce, risk and value realisation.

courses.title: Business Analytics
courses.summary: Data-driven insights for strategic and operational decision-making.
courses.description: A practice-oriented course that develops the ability to frame business questions, analyse data, interpret models and communicate evidence for managerial action.
courses.audience: Business management, engineering management and early-career professionals who need practical analytical decision skills.
courses.prerequisites: Basic spreadsheet skills; no advanced programming required.
outcomes: Frame business problems as answerable analytical questions.
Prepare and examine structured business data.
Select suitable descriptive, predictive and prescriptive approaches.
Interpret analytical results for managerial decisions.
Communicate recommendations through clear visual and written narratives.

modules.title: Business questions and analytical thinking
modules.description: Problem framing, decision context and measures of success.

modules.title: Data preparation and exploration
modules.description: Data quality, summaries, patterns and visual analysis.

modules.title: Predictive analysis for management
modules.description: Forecasting, classification and practical model interpretation.

modules.title: Decision models and scenarios
modules.description: What-if analysis, optimisation and uncertainty.

modules.title: Communicating analytical recommendations
modules.description: Dashboards, executive narratives and implementation choices.

courses.title: Business Science
courses.summary: Models and methods for solving complex business problems.
courses.description: An interdisciplinary course combining business understanding, analytical methods, experimentation and systems thinking to address complex organisational problems.
courses.audience: Students and professionals seeking an integrated approach to business problems that cross functions, data and organisational systems.
courses.prerequisites: Introductory business statistics or equivalent experience is helpful.
outcomes: Diagnose complex business problems using structured inquiry.
Combine quantitative and qualitative evidence.
Design experiments and evaluate managerial interventions.
Model interactions across business functions and systems.
Translate findings into feasible organisational action.

modules.title: Business science as an integrated discipline
modules.description: Problem contexts, evidence and model-based reasoning.

modules.title: Causal questions and experimentation
modules.description: Experiments, natural experiments and practical limitations.

modules.title: Systems and stakeholder analysis
modules.description: Interdependencies, feedback and organisational behaviour.

modules.title: Decision models under uncertainty
modules.description: Scenarios, trade-offs and robust choices.

modules.title: From evidence to implementation
modules.description: Recommendations, adoption and evaluation.

courses.title: Data-Driven Decision Making
courses.summary: From data to decisions with confidence and clarity.
courses.description: A managerial course on using data, evidence and structured judgement to improve decisions while recognising uncertainty, bias and organisational constraints.
courses.audience: Graduate and postgraduate students, managers and technical leaders responsible for evidence-based choices.
outcomes: Distinguish information, evidence and managerial judgement.
Define decision criteria and relevant measures.
Interpret uncertainty and common analytical limitations.
Challenge assumptions and reduce decision bias.
Present an evidence-based recommendation to stakeholders.

modules.title: Decision quality and evidence
modules.description: Decision frames, criteria and information value.

modules.title: Interpreting data and uncertainty
modules.description: Variation, confidence and practical significance.

modules.title: Bias, incentives and organisational context
modules.description: Judgement traps and stakeholder dynamics.

modules.title: Scenario and trade-off analysis
modules.description: Alternatives, sensitivity and robust choices.

modules.title: Communicating decisions
modules.description: Recommendation structure, visual evidence and action.

courses.title: Digital Transformation for Managers
courses.summary: Lead change and innovation in the digital era.
courses.description: A management course on digital strategy, operating-model transformation, technology choices, organisational change and value delivery.
courses.audience: Managers, technical leaders, consultants and graduate students responsible for digital change.
courses.prerequisites: Professional or academic exposure to organisational processes is helpful.
outcomes: Assess digital pressures and strategic opportunities.
Define a transformation ambition and portfolio of initiatives.
Evaluate operating-model and technology implications.
Plan governance, change and capability development.
Create a value-focused transformation roadmap.

modules.title: Digital strategy and competitive change
modules.description: Business models, ecosystems and strategic priorities.

modules.title: Customer, process and data transformation
modules.description: Experience, operations and enterprise information.

modules.title: Technology and operating-model choices
modules.description: Platforms, architecture, sourcing and ways of working.

modules.title: Leading organisational change
modules.description: Leadership, capabilities, culture and adoption.

modules.title: Transformation portfolio and value delivery
modules.description: Roadmaps, governance, measures and benefits.

courses.title: Operations Analytics
courses.summary: Optimise operations through analytics and modelling.
courses.description: An applied course on process analysis, capacity, forecasting, inventory, scheduling and optimisation for operational performance.
courses.audience: Operations, supply-chain, engineering management and business analytics students and professionals.
courses.prerequisites: Basic quantitative methods and spreadsheet modelling.
outcomes: Measure and diagnose operational performance.
Develop demand and workload forecasts.
Analyse capacity, queues, inventory and service levels.
Formulate scheduling and optimisation decisions.
Recommend operational improvements using analytical evidence.

modules.title: Operational performance and process data
modules.description: Flow, capacity, productivity and service measures.

modules.title: Forecasting for operations
modules.description: Demand, workload and forecast evaluation.

modules.title: Capacity, queues and service
modules.description: Congestion, resource decisions and service trade-offs.

modules.title: Inventory and scheduling analytics
modules.description: Policies, priorities and resource allocation.

modules.title: Optimisation and implementation
modules.description: Models, scenarios and operational adoption.
